summary.intent: Fix a bug in the MCP SSE transport where Streamable HTTP servers were incorrectly reported as 'dead' when returning HTTP 405 for GET requests.
summary.outcome: Bug fixed with a one-line change in SseTransport::is_alive() to treat any HTTP response as 'alive' (only network errors as 'dead'), with 6 unit tests and an E2E test all passing. PR #733 created and pushed.

repo: MCP transport logic lives in crates/mcp/src/sse_transport.rs; the is_alive() method determines server liveness
E2E tests for MCP UI flows live in crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/mcp.spec.js and use a mock MCP server at crates/web/ui/e2e/mock-mcp-server.js
The MCP spec makes GET support optional for Streamable HTTP servers — returning 405 is valid behavior, not an error

code.finding: is_alive() originally only accepted 2xx/401 status codes as 'alive'; fix changed it to treat any HTTP response as alive, reserving 'dead' for network-level errors only
New mock Streamable HTTP MCP server: JSON-RPC over POST, Bearer token auth, returns 405 for GET — used to reproduce the bug in tests

workflow: Tests were run in two passes: targeted is_alive() unit tests first (6 tests), then full crate suite (93 tests) to catch regressions
Session used a git worktree at /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/zesty-variety for isolated development

open_items: E2E test added but may need CI environment validation to confirm mock server lifecycle works reliably in automated pipelines
The mock-mcp-server.js reproduces only the 405 case — other Streamable HTTP edge cases (e.g. 403, redirects) could be tested further
